result.stdout:
Description:
  MokaDocs — A modern, beautiful, extensible static documentation site generator for .NET projects.

Usage:
  mokadocs [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  init      Scaffold a new MokaDocs project (mokadocs.yaml + docs/ folder)
  new       Scaffold new pages, plugins, or component examples
  build     Build the documentation site
  serve     Build and serve the site locally with hot reload
  clean     Delete the output directory
  info      Show environment and configuration information
  validate  Validate config, check links, and report warnings
  doctor    Diagnose issues in your documentation project
  stats     Show project statistics

commands.payload:
Description:
  MokaDocs — A modern, beautiful, extensible static documentation site generator for .NET projects.

Usage:
  mokadocs [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  init      Scaffold a new MokaDocs project (mokadocs.yaml + docs/ folder)
  new       Scaffold new pages, plugins, or component examples
  build     Build the documentation site
  serve     Build and serve the site locally with hot reload
  clean     Delete the output directory
  info      Show environment and configuration information
  validate  Validate config, check links, and report warnings
  doctor    Diagnose issues in your documentation project
  stats     Show project statistics

result.stdout:
Description:
  Build the documentation site

Usage:
  mokadocs build [options]

Options:
  --watch                  Watch for changes and rebuild automatically
  --config <config>        Path to configuration file (default: mokadocs.yaml)
  --output <output>        Output directory (default: _site/)
  --verbose                Enable verbose logging
  --draft                  Include draft pages
  --no-cache               Force full rebuild without caching
  --base-path <base-path>  Base path for subdirectory deployment (e.g., /Moka.Docs for GitHub Pages)
  -?, -h, --help           Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Build the documentation site

Usage:
  mokadocs build [options]

Options:
  --watch                  Watch for changes and rebuild automatically
  --config <config>        Path to configuration file (default: mokadocs.yaml)
  --output <output>        Output directory (default: _site/)
  --verbose                Enable verbose logging
  --draft                  Include draft pages
  --no-cache               Force full rebuild without caching
  --base-path <base-path>  Base path for subdirectory deployment (e.g., /Moka.Docs for GitHub Pages)
  -?, -h, --help           Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Diagnose issues in your documentation project

Usage:
  mokadocs doctor [options]

Options:
  --fix              Attempt to auto-fix issues (e.g., add missing front matter titles)
  --config <config>  Path to config file (default: mokadocs.yaml)
  --verbose          Show detailed output for each check
  -?, -h, --help     Show help and usage information

  --verbose          Show detailed output for each check

result.stdout:
Description:
  Scaffold new pages, plugins, or component examples

Usage:
  mokadocs new [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  page <name>       Create a new Markdown documentation page
  plugin <name>     Scaffold a new MokaDocs plugin project
  component <name>  Scaffold a Markdown page showcasing a component

commands.payload:
Description:
  Scaffold new pages, plugins, or component examples

Usage:
  mokadocs new [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  page <name>       Create a new Markdown documentation page
  plugin <name>     Scaffold a new MokaDocs plugin project
  component <name>  Scaffold a Markdown page showcasing a component

result.stdout:
Description:
  Scaffold a Markdown page showcasing a component

Usage:
  mokadocs new component <name> [options]

Arguments:
  <name>  Component type (card, steps, link-cards, code-group, changelog)

Options:
  --path <path>   Output directory [default: ./docs]
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:

Arguments:

result.stdout:
Description:
  Create a new Markdown documentation page

Usage:
  mokadocs new page <name> [options]

Arguments:
  <name>  Page name (e.g. getting-started, api-overview)

Options:
  --title <title>    Page title (defaults to name in Title Case)
  --path <path>      Output directory [default: ./docs]
  --layout <layout>  Layout template (default, landing, api-type) [default: default]
  --order <order>    Sort order number
  -?, -h, --help     Show help and usage information

commands.payload:

  --layout <layout>  Layout template (default, landing, api-type) [default: default]
  --order <order>    Sort order number
  -?, -h, --help     Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Scaffold a new MokaDocs plugin project

Usage:
  mokadocs new plugin <name> [options]

Arguments:
  <name>  Plugin name in kebab-case (e.g. my-custom-plugin)

Options:
  --path <path>   Output directory [default: .]
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Build and serve the site locally with hot reload

Usage:
  mokadocs serve [options]

Options:
  --port <port>      Port to serve on [default: 5080]
  --verbose          Enable verbose logging
  --config <config>  Path to configuration file (default: mokadocs.yaml)
  --output <output>  Output directory (default: _site/)
  --open             Open browser automatically
  --no-open          Don't open browser automatically
  -?, -h, --help     Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Show project statistics

Usage:
  mokadocs stats [options]

Options:
  --config <config>  Path to config file (default: mokadocs.yaml)
  --json             Output as JSON (for CI pipelines)
  -?, -h, --help     Show help and usage information
